insert into `core_news_translations` (`locale`, `origin_id`, `title`, `content`, `create_user`, `updated_at`, `created_at`) values ('en', 193, 'Motorbike trip to Cat Ba: Safety guide and rest stops.', '

Motorbike touring in Cat Ba is a journey of freedom, allowing you to actively explore the pristine beauty of Jade Island via the stunning cross-island road. To make your trip complete, you should travel along the Hanoi - Hai Phong route, take the Dong Bai ferry and follow safety rules when descending the passes, while stopping at spots such as Cat Ba National Park or the Cannon Fort.

 

Why should you choose motorbike touring in Cat Ba for your next vacation?

 

Cat Ba has always been an attractive destination, but motorbike touring in Cat Ba brings completely different emotions compared to taking a coach or speedboat. Freedom is the first factor; you can stop at any stretch of the mountain pass to take photos, breathe in the sea air or simply watch the sunset fall over Lan Ha Bay.

 

Moreover, the cross-island road of Cat Ba is dubbed one of the most beautiful coastal roads in the North. The feeling of riding on a road with majestic limestone mountains on one side and the deep blue sea on the other will be an unforgettable memory. Traveling on your own also helps you save on transportation costs on the island and gives you more flexibility to visit caves or beaches far from the center.

 

 

What is the route for motorbike touring in Cat Ba from Hanoi?

 

To begin your motorbike touring in Cat Ba journey, you have two main directions from Hanoi. Each route has its own characteristics in terms of scenery and travel time.

 

Traveling via the Hai Phong direction (Dong Bai ferry)

 

This is the most popular route for backpackers because of its optimal distance and convenient transportation system.



    • Hanoi - Hai Phong: You travel along old National Highway 5 (do not take the 5B expressway as motorbikes are banned). This stretch is about 100km long, taking around 2.5 - 3 hours.

 

    • Hai Phong - Dong Bai ferry: Go through Hai Phong city, heading towards the Dinh Vu - Cat Hai sea-crossing bridge. Note that the wind on this bridge is often very strong, so you need to keep a firm grip on the handlebars.

 

    • Dong Bai ferry - Cai Vieng terminal: Here, you buy tickets for both passengers and motorbikes to cross to the island. The ferry waiting time can be long on weekends, so plan to go early.

 

    • Cross-island road: After arriving at Cai Vieng terminal, you'll officially begin the journey of riding on the island to reach the town center.



Traveling via the Ha Long direction (Tuan Chau ferry)

 

This route is longer, but in return you'll get to admire the majestic scenery of Ha Long Bay before setting foot in Cat Ba.



    • From Hanoi, you go in the direction of Bac Ninh - Chi Linh (Hai Duong) - Dong Trieu - Ha Long.

 

    • Upon reaching Tuan Chau Island, you catch the Tuan Chau - Gia Luan ferry. The road from Gia Luan terminal to Cat Ba town passes through the National Park, very suitable for those who love the green of the forests and mountains.



 

 

 

What to prepare for safe and complete motorbike touring in Cat Ba?

 

A successful long trip always begins with thorough preparation. For motorbike touring in Cat Ba, you need to pay special attention to technical and health factors.

 

Checking the vehicle and maintenance



    • Change the oil and check the tires: Make sure the tires have good grip because the island roads have many sharp curves and can be slippery when it rains.

 

    • Brake system: Check both the front and rear brakes to ensure safety when descending the cross-island passes.

 

    • Lights and mirrors: Make sure the headlights work well if you intend to travel in the evening or early morning when there's fog.



Necessary personal items



    • Vehicle documents: A driver's license, vehicle registration and compulsory insurance are indispensable.

 

    • Clothing: A 3/4 or full-face helmet, gloves, a thin jacket to protect against the sun and sea wind.

 

    • Basic repair tools: A small toolkit including a hand pump, patches and screws will be very useful in case your vehicle breaks down on deserted stretches of road.



In addition, discover many more high-quality travel experiences at Vietnamtoure.com

 

 

Must-see stops when motorbike touring in Cat Ba

 

When riding on your motorbike touring in Cat Ba trip, you shouldn't focus only on the destination of the town. Take time to explore the stops along the way to fully appreciate the beauty of this place.

 

Cat Ba National Park

 

Located right on the cross-island road, this is an ideal stop for nature lovers. You can park your vehicle and take a short trek up to Ngu Lam Peak to take in the entire panorama of Jade Island from above.

 

Quan Y Cave (Hospital Cave)

 

A historical site located right by the main road. It was once a field hospital during the resistance war, with a unique vaulted structure inside the limestone mountain. Visiting Quan Y Cave makes your journey more meaningful and adds an element of historical discovery.

 

Cannon Fort (Height 177)

 

Although the road up is a bit steep, riding a motorbike up here will bring a worthy reward. From this position, you can take in the whole panorama of Lan Ha Bay and the fishing boats bobbing in the distance. This is also the most beautiful sunset-watching spot on the island.

 

Cat Co 1, 2 and 3 beaches

 

After a long day of riding, immersing yourself in the cool water at the Cat Co beaches is the most wonderful thing. These beaches are connected by a trail along the mountainside, where you can walk and admire the sea views quite serendipitously.

 

 

What should you note about safety when motorbike touring in Cat Ba?

 

Safety is always the top priority on every trip. When taking part in motorbike touring in Cat Ba, you need to keep in mind the following golden rules to avoid unfortunate risks.



    • Maintain a steady speed: The cross-island road is very beautiful but has many blind curves. Maintain a moderate speed and absolutely do not cross into the opposite lane.

 

    • Watch the weather: Avoid traveling on the island during heavy rain because the asphalt can be slippery and there's a risk of rockfall from the cliffs.

 

    • Be careful with the wind on the bridge: The Tan Vu - Lach Huyen bridge has very strong winds. If you feel your vehicle shaking, slow down and lower your center of gravity to increase road grip.

 

    • Do not use stimulants: Absolutely do not drink alcohol while driving to ensure safety for yourself and those around you.



 

 

 

Food and rest tips for backpackers in Cat Ba

 

Going motorbike touring in Cat Ba often comes with the desire to explore local cuisine in the most rustic way.

 

Cuisine you can't miss

 

Try specialties such as Cat Ba shrimp noodles, salt-roasted mantis shrimp or grilled geoduck with scallion oil. The Cat Ba market area is where many delicious eateries with affordable prices are concentrated, very suitable for backpackers.

 

Choosing accommodation

 

If you like a lively atmosphere, choose hotels and homestays right in the town center. Conversely, if you want a quiet space and to immerse yourself in nature, the homestay areas near the National Park or along Lan Ha Bay will be a great choice.

 

 

Step-by-step guide for motorbike touring in Cat Ba

 

To give you an overview, below is the process for carrying out the trip:



    • Make a plan: Choose the timing (best from April to October). Book accommodation in advance if going on a weekend.

 

    • Maintain the vehicle: Take your vehicle to the shop to check the tires, chain, brakes and change the engine oil 1-2 days before departure.

 

    • Pack your belongings: Prioritize compact, light items, bring a raincoat and sunscreen.

 

    • Travel: Set off early in the morning to avoid traffic jams in Hanoi and reduce ferry waiting time.

 

    • Experience: Ride leisurely on the cross-island road, stop at check-in spots and enjoy seafood.

 

    • Wrap up: Recheck your vehicle before riding the long road back to Hanoi.



Frequently asked questions about motorbike touring in Cat Ba

 

Q: Is there a fee to enter the island when motorbike touring in Cat Ba?

 

You don't pay a fee to enter the island, but you will have to pay the ferry fee for passengers and vehicles. The Dong Bai ferry ticket price for motorbikes is currently around 45,000 - 50,000 VND (including the driver).

 

Q: Are the roads on Cat Ba Island difficult to travel?

 

The asphalt roads on the island are very beautiful and smooth. However, there are some steep sections and hairpin bends that require the rider to have a steady hand and not be complacent.

 

Q: How many days are enough for motorbike touring in Cat Ba?

 

A 2-day, 1-night trip is enough for you to experience the basic spots. However, if you want to explore Lan Ha Bay more deeply and trek the forest, you should spend 3 days and 2 nights.

 

Q: Are there gas stations on the island?

 

There are many gas stations in the town center area and a few spots along the cross-island road. However, you should fill up the tank before starting the deserted routes deep in the forest.

 

 

The motorbike touring in Cat Ba journey is not just a trip, but also a way for you to rediscover balance after stressful working days. The combination of sea breeze, golden sunshine and winding roads will help your soul be "healed" in the most natural way.

 

If you're looking for a deeper trip that combines exploring nature and caring for your mental health, let us accompany you.

 

LIEN MINH VIET International Trading Company Limited

 

Virenaretreat - Where Your Soul Is Healed !

 

Address: 102 My Dinh, Cau Giay, Hanoi

 

Phone: 0986662282

 

Email: support@virenaretreat.com Website: virenaretreat.com

    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Q: Is there a fee to enter the island when motorbike touring in Cat Ba?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">You don't pay a fee to enter the island, but you will have to pay the ferry fee for passengers and vehicles. The Dong Bai ferry ticket price for motorbikes is currently around 45,000 - 50,000 VND (including the driver).</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Q: Are the roads on Cat Ba Island difficult to travel?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">The asphalt roads on the island are very beautiful and smooth. However, there are some steep sections and hairpin bends that require the rider to have a steady hand and not be complacent.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Q: How many days are enough for motorbike touring in Cat Ba?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">A 2-day, 1-night trip is enough for you to experience the basic spots. However, if you want to explore Lan Ha Bay more deeply and trek the forest, you should spend 3 days and 2 nights.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Q: Are there gas stations on the island?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">There are many gas stations in the town center area and a few spots along the cross-island road. However, you should fill up the tank before starting the deserted routes deep in the forest.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/phuot-cat-ba-bang-xe-may-huong-dan-an-toan-va-cac-diem-dung-chan-1.jpg" alt="" width="900" height="599" /></span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">The </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">motorbike touring in Cat Ba</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> journey is not just a trip, but also a way for you to rediscover balance after stressful working days. The combination of sea breeze, golden sunshine and winding roads will help your soul be "healed" in the most natural way.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">If you're looking for a deeper trip that combines exploring nature and caring for your mental health, let us accompany you.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.9871999999999999; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">LIEN MINH VIET International Trading Company Limited </span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.9871999999999999; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Virenaretreat - Where Your Soul Is Healed !</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.9871999999999999; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Address:</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> 102 My Dinh, Cau Giay, Hanoi</span></p>\r\n

    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.9871999999999999; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Phone:</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> 0986662282</span></p>\r\n
